fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Nebraska Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Nebraska - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity nebraska, how to transfer physical therapist license to nebraska, physical therapist compact nebraska, nebraska physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Nebraska DHHS — Physical Therapy
board.parent_dept: Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS)
board.mailing_address: Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services, Credentialing Division, P.O. Box 94986, Lincoln, NE 68509-4986

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Nebraska.

steps.title: Create Account and Submit Application
steps.description: Access the NE DHHS Online Application Portal at https://ne.gov/go/DHHS-Apply, create an account, and complete the Physical Therapist application form, selecting the appropriate application type (e.g., Initial Licensure by Exam or Licensure by Endorsement).

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your CAPTE-accredited physical therapy program to be sent directly to the Nebraska DHHS Credentialing Division. If applying by examination, ensure your official National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE) scores are sent directly from FSBPT. For endorsement, arrange for verification of all out-of-state licenses to be sent to DHHS.

steps.title: Pass Nebraska Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Complete and pass the Nebraska Jurisprudence Examination, which is typically accessed online after submitting your application through the portal.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided by DHHS to complete the required fingerprint-based criminal background check.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Nebraska physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $130. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Nebraska have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Nebraska participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Nebraska without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Nebraska?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Nebraska while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Nebraska
hero.dek: Physical Therapist licensure in Nebraska is processed through the NE DHHS Online Application Portal, which handles all applications for initial licensure and endorsement.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: To begin, visit the NE DHHS Online Application Portal and click 'Sign Up Now' to create a new user account. Once registered, log in with your User ID and Password to select the Physical Therapy profession and initiate your application.
